title: Adapting to Industry Terminology
concept: Pretrained models struggle on specialized fields (medical, legal, finance) due to vocabulary shifts, requiring domain-specific fine-tuning.

exercises.prompt: What kind of data are standard pretrained language models mostly trained on?
exercises.explain: Pretrained models are built using massive amounts of general internet text, which doesn't capture the deep, specialized nuances of professional fields.

cards.title: Lost in Translation
cards.body: Imagine asking an AI to read a brain scan report, but it treats the medical jargon like standard internet slang. In professional fields, using the wrong word isn't just an typo—it can be a critical error. Let's see how we teach AI to speak like a specialist!

cards.title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning
cards.body: Pretrained AI models are trained on general internet text. When they encounter specialized industries like medicine, law, or finance, they struggle with industry terminology. Domain-specific fine-tuning is the process of training these models on specialized texts so they can master professional jargon.

cards.title: The General Translator
cards.body: Imagine buying a general-purpose translator app and taking it to a high-tech medical conference. The app knows everyday words, but it gets completely confused when doctors start talking about specific diseases and treatments. To make it useful, you have to upload a medical dictionary. That is what fine-tuning does for AI!

cards.title: Executing a Contract
cards.body: A standard AI model might read the legal phrase 'execute a contract' and think it means to destroy or kill the document. After domain-specific fine-tuning on legal texts, the model correctly understands that 'execute' means to sign and make the contract legally binding.

cards.title: The 'Omniscient' AI Myth
cards.body: Many people believe that because an AI is 'smart' and has read the whole internet, it already understands professional fields perfectly. In reality, general internet text doesn't contain enough deep, specialized knowledge, meaning general models often hallucinate or guess incorrectly when faced with real industry documents.

cards.title: Word Meanings Shift
cards.body: See how a general model and a domain-tuned model interpret the exact same professional sentence differently.

right.text: Reads 'fiat' and correctly understands it as a legal decree or order.

left.text: Reads 'fiat' and thinks of the Italian car brand.

exercises.prompt: Why do standard pretrained models often struggle when first used in medical or legal settings?

options.text: They are too small to fit industry documents in their memory.
They lack exposure to the specialized vocabulary and unique jargon of those fields.
They refuse to process professional documents due to copyright laws.
They can only read text that is written in simple, conversational English.

exercises.explain: Pretrained models are trained on general texts and lack deep exposure to the unique terms, jargon, and vocabulary shifts found in specialized industries like law and medicine.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The same word can have different meanings in general conversation compared to specialized industries.
exercises.explain: A word can have a completely different, highly specific meaning in a professional field compared to its everyday usage.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing concept to complete the sentence below.
exercises.sentence: To help a general AI model learn the complex language of finance, we use domain-specific ____________.
exercises.explain: Domain-specific fine-tuning is the process of training a general model on industry-specific texts to help it master professional vocabulary.

exercises.prompt: Which response shows an AI model that has been successfully fine-tuned on legal terminology?

options.text: AI Model A: "The phrase 'execute the contract' means we must delete and destroy the document immediately."
AI Model B: "The phrase 'execute the contract' means to sign it and make it legally active."
AI Model C: "The phrase 'execute the contract' means to write a computer program that prints the contract."

exercises.explain: AI Model B displays a clear understanding of legal terminology ('execute' meaning to sign/validate), whereas AI Model A takes the literal, non-legal meaning of the word.

exercises.prompt: Why can't we just deploy a standard, pretrained language model directly into a hospital or a courthouse without any extra training?

options.text: They struggle with specialized vocabulary and jargon used by industry professionals.
They completely forget how to speak English or generate readable sentences.
They automatically update themselves by searching the live web during a chat.
They are blocked by security systems from reading medical and legal documents.

exercises.explain: General models are trained on everyday internet text. When they enter high-stakes professional fields like medicine, law, or finance, they must be fine-tuned on those specific vocabularies to understand the specialized terminology and prevent costly misunderstandings.
